title: fix XDG_RUNTIME_DIR for authfile
body:
fix bug https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1798343
log/logout fails after running `unset XDG_RUNTIME_DIR`. This patch calls getStore() to set XDG_RUNTIME_DIR in the code.

Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body: Could you add a test to make sure this works and does not regress?

Definitely please add a test for the regression.
I've a couple of concerns.  1).  If the user meant to disable that envvar, we turn it on for them again without them knowing.  I think we ought to either just print out a message like:

"The XDG_RUNTIME_DIR environment is undefined and must be set for buildah login to complete"

or before setting it, write out a message like:

"The XDG_RUNTIME_DIR environment variables is undefined, setting it to {x}"

to let the user know it's being set.

2) The code in getStore() does a lot of stuff.  Can we pull out the code that does the setting of XDG_RUNTIME_DIR into a small function that could be called from both places?

> Definitely please add a test for the regression.
> I've a couple of concerns. 1). If the user meant to disable that envvar, we turn it on for them again without them knowing. I think we ought to either just print out a message like:
> 
No this only turns it on for the podman command, it does not effect any other session of the user.  It is pretty much hidden from him, and just allows container/image to work correctly.
> "The XDG_RUNTIME_DIR environment is undefined and must be set for buildah login to complete"
> 
> or before setting it, write out a message like:
> 
> "The XDG_RUNTIME_DIR environment variables is undefined, setting it to {x}"
> 
> to let the user know it's being set.
> 
I don't think this is needed, since the user will never see a change.
>     1. The code in getStore() does a lot of stuff.  Can we pull out the code that does the setting of

This sounds like a good idea.

@rhatdan FWIW, we do a os.SetEnv() for the XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, so it would set it for the session, not just internally within Podman.  It will go away when they log out.  If you think it's good without a warning, I'm fine with that.
No,  Calling setenv inside of a program only changes the process and its children not for its parents.
LGTM all kinds of test unhappiness though.  Is bors kicking up its heals?
@cevich any thoughts on the next step to make bors happy here?

> any thoughts on the next step to make bors happy here?

Yes patients:  Wait for #2115 to merge first (it's about to) then the automatic retry should work fine.
This PR was included in a batch with a merge conflict, it will be automatically retried
If it fails again, I would recommend rebasing against the latest master.
@QiWang19 BTW, there is no more "bot", it's now called "bors".  I added documentation for it https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/2129 should merge soon.
